name: DNSRecon
overview: DNSRecon is a high-utility open source offensive security tool deployed for red teaming operations, active vulnerability assessment, and threat emulation.

core_modules.name: STANDARD_ENUM
core_modules.title: Standard DNS Record Lookup
core_modules.description: Queries NS, A, AAAA, MX, and TXT records of the target domain to build a basic zone outline.

core_modules.name: ZONE_TRANSFER
core_modules.title: Zone Transfer (AXFR) Tester
core_modules.description: Queries authoritative nameservers for zone transfer vulnerability, attempting to download the complete zone mapping.

steps.name: RUN HELP
steps.desc: Confirm DNSRecon compiles and prints options.

scenarios.name: Test 1: SRV Record Enumeration
scenarios.script:
dnsrecon -d google.com -t srv
target_host: The hostname providing the service

keyTakeaways.title: TEST_1
keyTakeaways.content: SRV Record Enumeration

keyTakeaways.title: EACH_LINE_IN_THE_OUTPUT_F
keyTakeaways.content: SRV _service._protocol.domain target_host IP_address port

keyTakeaways.title: SRV
keyTakeaways.content: Indicates this is a Service record

keyTakeaways.title: _SERVICE._PROTOCOL
keyTakeaways.content: The service name and protocol (e.g., _ldap._tcp)

keyTakeaways.title: DOMAIN
keyTakeaways.content: The domain being queried (google.com)

keyTakeaways.title: IP_ADDRESS
keyTakeaways.content: IPv4 or IPv6 address of the target host

keyTakeaways.title: PORT
keyTakeaways.content: TCP/UDP port number where the service is accessible

keyTakeaways.title: SERVICE_ENUMERATION
keyTakeaways.content: The scan reveals active services and their entry points

keyTakeaways.title: NETWORK_TOPOLOGY
keyTakeaways.content: Shows how Google's services are distributed and addressed

keyTakeaways.title: DUAL-STACK_IMPLEMENTATION
keyTakeaways.content: Demonstrates Google's IPv4/IPv6 infrastructure

keyTakeaways.title: SERVICE_FINGERPRINTING
keyTakeaways.content: Knowledge of exact services can help attackers focus their efforts

keyTakeaways.title: VERSION_INFORMATION
keyTakeaways.content: Service types can sometimes be correlated with software versions

keyTakeaways.title: AUTHENTICATION_PROBING
keyTakeaways.content: Services like LDAP might be targeted for authentication tests

keyTakeaways.title: SERVICE-SPECIFIC_VULNERAB
keyTakeaways.content: Knowing service types allows for targeted vulnerability scanning

keyTakeaways.title: RECONNAISSANCE_PHASE
keyTakeaways.content: Essential first step in mapping attack surface

keyTakeaways.title: TARGET_PRIORITIZATION
keyTakeaways.content: Helps identify potentially vulnerable services

keyTakeaways.title: LATERAL_MOVEMENT_PLANNING
keyTakeaways.content: Understanding service relationships assists in planning pivots

keyTakeaways.title: SOCIAL_ENGINEERING_PREPAR
keyTakeaways.content: Knowledge of service structure can make phishing more convincing

scenarios.name: Test 2: Wildcard Resolution Testing
scenarios.script:
dnsrecon -d google.com -t std -w
This scan shows the results of a general DNS enumeration against google.com using the command dnsrecon -d google.com -t std -w. It reveals multiple record types including SOA, NS, MX, A, AAAA, and TXT records.
dnsrecon Command Options Explained

keyTakeaways.title: TEST_2
keyTakeaways.content: Wildcard Resolution Testing

keyTakeaways.title: IMAGE_1
keyTakeaways.content: Extended SRV Records Analysis

keyTakeaways.title: IMAGE_2
keyTakeaways.content: Standard DNS Enumeration

keyTakeaways.title: DNSSEC_NOT_CONFIGURED
keyTakeaways.content: The red warning indicates Google.com doesn't have DNSSEC configured, which is a security extension for DNS.

keyTakeaways.title: LDAP_ENUMERATION
keyTakeaways.content: Test for anonymous binds or information leakage

keyTakeaways.title: SERVICE_VERSION_PROBING
keyTakeaways.content: Target specific services for version-specific vulnerabilities

keyTakeaways.title: DNS_CACHE_POISONING
keyTakeaways.content: Absence of DNSSEC increases risk on certain networks

keyTakeaways.title: MAIL_SERVER_TESTING
keyTakeaways.content: Probe for mail server vulnerabilities or misconfigurations

keyTakeaways.title: -D_DOMAIN
keyTakeaways.content: Specifies the target domain

keyTakeaways.title: -T_STD
keyTakeaways.content: Standard record enumeration (A, AAAA, MX, SOA, etc.)

keyTakeaways.title: -T_SRV
keyTakeaways.content: Service record enumeration only

scenarios.name: Test 3: DNSSEC Analysis: (using Dig tool)
scenarios.script:
dig +dnssec testphp.vulnweb.com
dig +dnssec testphp.vulnweb.com - Performs a DNS lookup requesting DNSSEC information for testphp.vulnweb.com

keyTakeaways.title: TEST_3
keyTakeaways.content: DNSSEC Analysis: (using Dig tool)

keyTakeaways.title: NO_DNSSEC_SIGNATURES
keyTakeaways.content: The output doesn't show any RRSIG (Resource Record Signature) records, indicating DNSSEC is not implemented for this domain.

keyTakeaways.title: NO_DS_RECORDS
keyTakeaways.content: No Delegation Signer records are present, confirming DNSSEC is not configured.

keyTakeaways.title: NO_DNSKEY_RECORDS
keyTakeaways.content: DNSKEY records (which would contain public keys for DNSSEC validation) are absent.

keyTakeaways.title: DNS_SPOOFING_RISK
keyTakeaways.content: Without DNSSEC, this domain is vulnerable to DNS cache poisoning and spoofing attacks.

keyTakeaways.title: MAN-IN-THE-MIDDLE_POTENTI
keyTakeaways.content: An attacker could potentially intercept DNS queries and redirect traffic to malicious servers.

keyTakeaways.title: TRUST_CHAIN_ISSUES
keyTakeaways.content: If this is a security-critical domain, lack of DNSSEC breaks the chain of trust in the DNS hierarchy.

keyTakeaways.title: EXTREMELY_LOW_TTL
keyTakeaways.content: The TTL value of 5 seconds is unusually low, which could indicate:

keyTakeaways.title: LOCAL_DNS_SERVER
keyTakeaways.content: The query was answered by 192.168.112.2, a local network DNS server, not directly from authoritative nameservers.

keyTakeaways.title: DNS_SPOOFING
keyTakeaways.content: Without DNSSEC, the domain is vulnerable to various DNS spoofing techniques.

keyTakeaways.title: IP_RECONNAISSANCE
keyTakeaways.content: The identified IP (44.228.249.3) can be subjected to further scanning and enumeration.

keyTakeaways.title: DNS_CACHE_POISONING
keyTakeaways.content: With no DNSSEC validation, recursive DNS servers handling this domain could be vulnerable to cache poisoning.

scenarios.name: Test 1: Enumerating Domain MX Records with SPF Check using dig
objectiveList: Uses dig with +short option to display concise output
Specifically targets MX and TXT records
scenarios.script: echo "[MX Records]"; dig testphp.vulnweb.com MX +short; echo "[SPF Records]"; dig testphp.vulnweb.com TXT +short | grep spf

keyTakeaways.title: TEST_1
keyTakeaways.content: Enumerating Domain MX Records with SPF Check using dig

keyTakeaways.title: MX_RECORDS
keyTakeaways.content: Identify mail servers responsible for receiving email

keyTakeaways.title: SPF_RECORDS
keyTakeaways.content: Define authorized mail servers allowed to send emails on behalf of the domain

keyTakeaways.title: "V=SPF1_INCLUDE
keyTakeaways.content: _spf.google.com ~all"

keyTakeaways.title: INCLUDE
keyTakeaways.content: _spf.google.com

keyTakeaways.title: INCLUDE
keyTakeaways.content: _spf.google.com: Instead of listing IPs directly, Google uses a dedicated subdomain to maintain its sender list

keyTakeaways.title: ~ALL_(HIGHLIGHTED_IN_RED)
keyTakeaways.content: Implements a "soft fail" policy rather than strict rejection (-all)

keyTakeaways.title: SPF_IMPLEMENTATION
keyTakeaways.content: Google has implemented SPF, but with a soft fail policy (~all)

keyTakeaways.title: DELEGATE_STRUCTURE
keyTakeaways.content: Uses include mechanism to reference another record (_spf.google.com)

keyTakeaways.title: MISSING_RECORDS
keyTakeaways.content: No visible DKIM or DMARC records in this particular query output

keyTakeaways.title: SPF_SOFT_FAIL
keyTakeaways.content: The ~all directive means non-compliant emails may still be delivered but flagged

keyTakeaways.title: SOCIAL_ENGINEERING
keyTakeaways.content: Understanding Google's email infrastructure aids in creating more convincing phishing attempts

keyTakeaways.title: SUBDOMAIN_ANALYSIS
keyTakeaways.content: The _spf.google.com subdomain could be further investigated for additional information

attackPaths.title: PERIMETER_PROBING
attackPaths.desc: Pivot internal scan actions targeting the newly resolved endpoints.

scenarios.name: Security Action Scenario 16
scenarios.objective: Test email delivery from unauthorized sources to assess how strictly the ~all policy is enforced
objectiveList: Test email delivery from unauthorized sources to assess how strictly the ~all policy is enforced

keyTakeaways.title: STRENGTHEN_SPF_POLICY
keyTakeaways.content: Consider upgrading from soft fail (~all) to hard fail (-all) for stronger protection

keyTakeaways.title: IMPLEMENT_DKIM
keyTakeaways.content: Ensure Domain Keys Identified Mail is configured for message signing

keyTakeaways.title: DEPLOY_DMARC
keyTakeaways.content: Add a DMARC policy that specifies handling instructions for SPF/DKIM failures

keyTakeaways.title: MONITOR_MAIL_SERVERS
keyTakeaways.content: Regularly check that listed mail servers remain under organizational control

keyTakeaways.title: SPF_RECORD_MAINTENANCE
keyTakeaways.content: Ensure the delegated _spf.google.com record is kept up-to-date with authorized senders

keyTakeaways.title: SPF
keyTakeaways.content: Verifies sending server IP addresses

keyTakeaways.title: DKIM
keyTakeaways.content: Cryptographically signs messages to verify they haven't been tampered with

keyTakeaways.title: DMARC
keyTakeaways.content: Provides policy instructions on how to handle messages that fail SPF/DKIM checks

attackPaths.title: PERIMETER_PROBING
attackPaths.desc: Pivot internal scan actions targeting the newly resolved endpoints.

keyTakeaways.content: Cache Snooping

keyTakeaways.content: DNS cache snooping

keyTakeaways.title: DOMAIN_POPULARITY_ASSESSM
keyTakeaways.content: Records found in Google's DNS cache suggest recent visits by other users

keyTakeaways.title: SUBDOMAIN_DISCOVERY
keyTakeaways.content: May reveal active but unpublished subdomains that others have queried

keyTakeaways.title: SERVICE_USAGE_PATTERNS
keyTakeaways.content: Cached records can indicate which services of the target domain are most frequently accessed

keyTakeaways.title: RECONNAISSANCE
keyTakeaways.content: Identify active subdomains without directly interacting with the target's servers

keyTakeaways.title: TRAFFIC_ANALYSIS
keyTakeaways.content: Infer which services or subdomains are most frequently used, indicating valuable targets

keyTakeaways.title: ATTACK_TARGETING
keyTakeaways.content: Focus exploitation efforts on services that appear to be actively used by legitimate users

keyTakeaways.title: PRIVACY_CONCERNS
keyTakeaways.content: DNS cache snooping can reveal user behavior patterns without their knowledge

keyTakeaways.title: INFORMATION_LEAKAGE
keyTakeaways.content: May expose internal subdomains or services not intended for public knowledge

keyTakeaways.title: TARGETING_DATA
keyTakeaways.content: Provides attackers with intelligence for more focused attacks on popular services

keyTakeaways.title: MULTIPLE_DNS_SERVERS
keyTakeaways.content: Test against various public DNS providers (Google, Cloudflare, etc.)

keyTakeaways.title: CUSTOM_DICTIONARY
keyTakeaways.content: Use specialized wordlists for thorough subdomain discovery

keyTakeaways.title: RECORD_TYPE_SPECIFICATION
keyTakeaways.content: Target specific DNS record types

keyTakeaways.title: CACHED_RECORDS
keyTakeaways.content: Indicate recent queries by other users (suggesting active usage)

keyTakeaways.title: TTL_VALUES
keyTakeaways.content: Lower remaining TTL values suggest more recent queries

keyTakeaways.title: RECORD_DISTRIBUTION
keyTakeaways.content: Pattern of which records are cached can reveal service usage profiles

keyTakeaways.title: DNS_QUERY_PRIVACY
keyTakeaways.content: Use DNS over HTTPS (DoH) or DNS over TLS (DoT) to encrypt DNS queries

keyTakeaways.title: INTERNAL_DOMAIN_NAMING
keyTakeaways.content: Avoid revealing internal structure in DNS naming schemes

keyTakeaways.title: DNS_CACHE_MANAGEMENT
keyTakeaways.content: For sensitive DNS servers, consider shorter TTL values

keyTakeaways.title: SPLIT-HORIZON_DNS
keyTakeaways.content: Use different DNS responses for internal vs external queries

keyTakeaways.title: DNS_TRAFFIC_MONITORING
keyTakeaways.content: Monitor for patterns of non-recursive queries that may indicate cache snooping

keyTakeaways.title: SECTION_5
keyTakeaways.content: Advanced Techniques and Reporting

scenarios.name: Test 1: Comprehensive Scan with JSON Output

keyTakeaways.title: TEST_1
keyTakeaways.content: Comprehensive Scan with JSON Output

keyTakeaways.title: TECHNIQUES
keyTakeaways.content: standard lookup, reverse lookup, brute force

keyTakeaways.title: DNSSEC
keyTakeaways.content: Not configured (potential security concern)

keyTakeaways.title: NAMESERVERS
keyTakeaways.content: Four authoritative nameservers (ns1-ns4.google.com) with both IPv4 and IPv6 addresses

keyTakeaways.title: MAIL_SERVERS
keyTakeaways.content: Multiple MX records pointing to smtp.google.com with varying priorities

keyTakeaways.title: EMAIL_SECURITY
keyTakeaways.content: DMARC record configured with a "reject" policy for failed authentication

keyTakeaways.title: REPORTING
keyTakeaways.content: Email authentication reports directed to mailauth-reports@google.com

keyTakeaways.title: DISCOVERED_SRV_RECORDS
keyTakeaways.content: 8 service records found, including:

keyTakeaways.title: CORE_SUBDOMAINS
keyTakeaways.content: Numerous subdomains discovered through brute force, including:

keyTakeaways.title: CNAME_RECORDS
keyTakeaways.content: Multiple subdomains are aliases:

keyTakeaways.title: EXPANSIVE_SERVICE_FOOTPRI
keyTakeaways.content: Google operates numerous subdomains, each representing a potential entry point

keyTakeaways.title: SERVICE_DIVERSITY
keyTakeaways.content: Different services (LDAP, CalDAV, etc.) may have varying security implementations

keyTakeaways.title: GEOGRAPHIC_DISTRIBUTION
keyTakeaways.content: IP addresses span multiple subnets, indicating distributed infrastructure

keyTakeaways.title: DNSSEC_MISSING
keyTakeaways.content: The absence of DNSSEC presents potential DNS spoofing opportunities

keyTakeaways.title: ROBUST_EMAIL_SECURITY
keyTakeaways.content: Strong DMARC policy ("p=reject") shows mature email security practices

keyTakeaways.title: CANONICAL_NAME_CHAINS
keyTakeaways.content: Multiple CNAME redirections reveal actual infrastructure behind services

keyTakeaways.title: IPV6_IMPLEMENTATION
keyTakeaways.content: Dual-stack deployment across all services

keyTakeaways.title: SUBDOMAIN_TARGETING
keyTakeaways.content: Focus on less mainstream subdomains that might have weaker security

keyTakeaways.title: SERVICE-SPECIFIC_TESTING
keyTakeaways.content: Target specific services like LDAP (port 389) for service-specific vulnerabilities

keyTakeaways.title: CANONICAL_NAME_ANALYSIS
keyTakeaways.content: Track CNAME chains to identify actual hosting infrastructure

keyTakeaways.title: ERROR_MESSAGE_ANALYSIS
keyTakeaways.content: The "Invalid Address/CIDR" error suggests improper reverse lookup configuration

keyTakeaways.title: LOAD_BALANCING
keyTakeaways.content: Multiple IP addresses for key services suggest load balancing

keyTakeaways.title: SERVICE_CONSOLIDATION
keyTakeaways.content: Many subdomains canonically pointing to www3.l.google.com indicates frontend consolidation

keyTakeaways.title: SERVICE_SEGREGATION
keyTakeaways.content: Different service types (accounts, ads, ai) on different subdomains and IP addresses

keyTakeaways.title: HIERARCHICAL_NAMING
keyTakeaways.content: Logical subdomain structure reflects service organization

keyTakeaways.title: REDUNDANT_NAMESERVERS
keyTakeaways.content: Four authoritative nameservers for reliability

keyTakeaways.title: CLOUD_INFRASTRUCTURE
keyTakeaways.content: IPv6 addresses follow Google Cloud format patterns

keyTakeaways.title: ZONE_TRANSFER_ATTEMPTS
keyTakeaways.content: Test for misconfigured DNS servers allowing zone transfers

keyTakeaways.title: ADDITIONAL_RECORD_TYPES
keyTakeaways.content: Query for less common record types (CAA, SSHFP, etc.)

keyTakeaways.title: HISTORICAL_DNS_ANALYSIS
keyTakeaways.content: Compare current results with historical DNS data to identify infrastructure changes

keyTakeaways.title: SUBDOMAIN_TAKEOVER
keyTakeaways.content: Check for subdomain takeover vulnerabilities in discovered CNAMEs

keyTakeaways.title: DNS_CACHE_POISONING
keyTakeaways.content: Test for susceptibility to cache poisoning due to missing DNSSEC

keyTakeaways.title: SERVICE-SPECIFIC_PROBING
keyTakeaways.content: Test discovered services (LDAP, CalDAV) for misconfigurations

keyTakeaways.title: IMPLEMENT_DNSSEC
keyTakeaways.content: Add DNSSEC to prevent DNS spoofing attacks

keyTakeaways.title: SERVICE_CONSOLIDATION_REV
keyTakeaways.content: Evaluate the security implications of numerous services sharing hosts

keyTakeaways.title: CNAME_CHAIN_REDUCTION
keyTakeaways.content: Simplify CNAME chains to reduce complexity and potential points of failure

keyTakeaways.title: SUBDOMAIN_INVENTORY
keyTakeaways.content: Maintain accurate inventory of all subdomains to prevent forgotten assets

keyTakeaways.title: RECORD_TTL_STANDARDIZATIO
keyTakeaways.content: Implement consistent TTL policies across record types

attackPaths.title: PERIMETER_PROBING

scenarios.name: Test 2: Targeted Zone Transfer with Custom Nameserver

keyTakeaways.title: TEST_2
keyTakeaways.content: Targeted Zone Transfer with Custom Nameserver

keyTakeaways.title: TECHNIQUE
keyTakeaways.content: zone transfer testing

keyTakeaways.title: FAILED_ZONE_TRANSFER
keyTakeaways.content: The scan shows "axfr: No records were returned" in red, indicating the zone transfer attempt was unsuccessful

keyTakeaways.title: PRE-STEPS_COMPLETED
keyTakeaways.content: The tool successfully resolved SOA and NS records for the domain

keyTakeaways.title: SECURITY_CONTROL_WORKING
keyTakeaways.content: The nameserver is correctly configured to deny zone transfers

keyTakeaways.title: DOMAIN_VALIDATION
keyTakeaways.content: Confirms zonetransfer.me is a valid domain with proper DNS configuration

keyTakeaways.title: NAMESERVER_IDENTIFICATION
keyTakeaways.content: Identifies nsztm1.digi.ninja as one of the authoritative nameservers

keyTakeaways.title: SECURITY_POSTURE
keyTakeaways.content: Indicates the domain has this specific security control properly implemented

keyTakeaways.title: RELATED_DOMAINS
keyTakeaways.content: Test similarly named domains that might share infrastructure

keyTakeaways.title: SUBDOMAIN_ENUMERATION
keyTakeaways.content: Since zone transfer failed, pivot to subdomain enumeration

keyTakeaways.title: ZONETRANSFER.ME
keyTakeaways.content: This domain is actually a deliberately vulnerable test domain created by Robin Wood (DigiNinja)

keyTakeaways.title: EXPECTED_BEHAVIOR
keyTakeaways.content: Normally this domain should ALLOW zone transfers for educational purposes

keyTakeaways.title: UNEXPECTED_RESULT
keyTakeaways.content: The failure shown here is unusual and might indicate:

keyTakeaways.title: ZONE_TRANSFER_RESTRICTION
keyTakeaways.content: Always restrict zone transfers to authorized IP addresses only

keyTakeaways.title: TSIG_IMPLEMENTATION
keyTakeaways.content: Use Transaction Signatures for secure zone transfers

keyTakeaways.title: SPLIT-DNS_ARCHITECTURE
keyTakeaways.content: Use separate external and internal DNS servers

keyTakeaways.title: DNS_TRAFFIC_MONITORING
keyTakeaways.content: Monitor for AXFR request attempts as potential reconnaissance indicators

keyTakeaways.title: REGULAR_TESTING
keyTakeaways.content: Conduct regular zone transfer tests to ensure configuration hasn't been accidentally changed

keyTakeaways.title: SECTION_7
keyTakeaways.content: Stealthy Reconnaissance

scenarios.name: Test 1: Low and Slow Subdomain Enumeration
scenarios.objective: Running brute force subdomain enumeration using top 1000 common subdomains list
objectiveList: Running brute force subdomain enumeration using top 1000 common subdomains list
Default wordlist being used for subdomain discovery
scenarios.script:
dnsrecon -d testphp.vulnweb.com -t brt -D /usr/share/dnsrecon/subdomains-top1000.txt --threads 1 --lifetime 5 -v
dnsrecon -d testphp.vulnweb.com -t brt -D /usr/share/dnsrecon/subdomains-top1000.txt --threads 1 --lifetime 5 -v

keyTakeaways.title: TEST_1
keyTakeaways.content: Low and Slow Subdomain Enumeration

keyTakeaways.title: USING_THE_DICTIONARY_FILE
keyTakeaways.content: /usr/share/dnsrecon/dnsrecon/data/namelist.txt (provided by tool)

keyTakeaways.title: BRT
keyTakeaways.content: Performing host and subdomain brute force against testphp.vulnweb.com...

keyTakeaways.title: METHODOLOGY
keyTakeaways.content: The tool is systematically testing common subdomain prefixes (0, 01, 02, 3com, a, about, etc.).

keyTakeaways.title: WORDLIST_SELECTION
keyTakeaways.content: Using a predefined list of top 1000 subdomains, which is a standard approach for initial reconnaissance.

keyTakeaways.title: SEQUENTIAL_TESTING
keyTakeaways.content: The scan follows a methodical approach, testing numeric prefixes, alphanumeric combinations, and common words like "about."

keyTakeaways.title: SCAN_CONFIGURATION
keyTakeaways.content: Using conservative settings (1 thread, 5-second timeout) to avoid overwhelming the target or triggering defenses.

keyTakeaways.title: SUBDOMAIN_DISCOVERY
keyTakeaways.content: Finding valid subdomains can reveal forgotten or unpatched systems that offer easier entry points.

keyTakeaways.title: INFRASTRUCTURE_MAPPING
keyTakeaways.content: Even without successful discoveries, this process helps map the target's DNS configuration and security posture.

attackPaths.title: PERIMETER_PROBING
attackPaths.desc: Pivot internal scan actions targeting the newly resolved endpoints.

troubleshooting.problem: DNSSEC validation warning printed in red
troubleshooting.cause: The target domain does not support DNSSEC signatures.
troubleshooting.resolution: Implement DNSSEC at the target domain's DNS registrar.

troubleshooting.problem: Zone transfer (AXFR) returns all failed / Refused
troubleshooting.cause: Nameservers are properly configured to deny zone transfers to arbitrary IPs.
troubleshooting.resolution: This is the expected secure state. No action needed unless testing internal nameservers.

security: Ensure all execution requests with DNSRecon are performed within authorized scope. Log all output artifacts securely and protect the integrity of local credential wordlists.
